title: carex nivalis f. cinnamomea
intro: Carex nivalis f. cinnamomea, also known as the golden sedge, is a subspecies of Carex nivalis, a type of sedge plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing swelling and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection. Carex nivalis f. cinnamomea is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, and can be found in some traditional remedies for skin and wound care.
